title: Oxalis cuernavacana
taxonomy: Oxalis cuernavacana is a species of plant in the family Oxalidaceae. It belongs to the genus Oxalis, which includes over 800 species of plants commonly known as wood sorrels. Within the Oxalis genus, Oxalis cuernavacana is classified as a member of the Oxalis subgenus. The plant is further divided into the species Oxalis cuernavacana, with no known subspecies. The classification of Oxalis cuernavacana reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Oxalidaceae family.

intro: Oxalis cuernavacana is a perennial plant species within the Oxalis genus, commonly known as a type of wood sorrel. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections.
